claims.text: 2,000 paying developers and $30K MRR three months after launch.

evidence.snippet: agentstack/runtime — 40 stars, repository created 3 weeks ago, 12 commits, no releases tagged.
Page not found. The site currently shows only a waitlist form; no pricing tiers or checkout are published.
Show HN: AgentStack (posted 18 days ago) — 6 points, 2 comments. Commenters ask when it will be usable.

trust.note: The claim of having 2,000 paying developers and $30K MRR is contradicted by the evidence. The GitHub repository is only 3 weeks old with minimal activity, the website lacks a pricing page or checkout system, and the Hacker News post indicates the product is not yet usable. These factors make the claimed traction implausible.

claims.text: Fastest-growing agent runtime; 3x week-over-week active workflows.

trust.note: The evidence does not provide any information about the growth rate or active workflows, so the claim cannot be verified or contradicted.

claims.text: Runtime supports parallel tool-calling agents with sub-second scheduling.

evidence.snippet: README describes a scheduler and tool-calling abstraction; example scripts present, no benchmarks published.

trust.note: The README mentions a scheduler and tool-calling abstraction, but there are no benchmarks or specific details confirming sub-second scheduling.

claims.text: Multi-agent orchestration is an emerging category with many entrants.

evidence.snippet: Survey lists a dozen agent-orchestration frameworks launched in the past year, most open-source and pre-revenue.

trust.note: The evidence lists a dozen agent-orchestration frameworks launched recently, supporting the claim that it's an emerging category with many entrants.
